ST_Contract10_PactOfOberon.Conrtact10.Clue02_Facts: An unseen opening somewhere in the Tower is causing concern for security.
ST_Contract10_PactOfOberon.Contract10.Title: Artistic License
ST_Contract10_PactOfOberon.Contract10.Outro:
Esteemed Colleague:
The Guildmistress sends her regards. Stealing "The Pact of Oberon" was no mean feat. It is an old painting and apparently recounts the arrival of Kilcairn's Wardstones around two thousand years ago. The Wardstones were supposedly a powerful gift from the Fey. As you may have observed on the artist's rendition, each of the stones has a symbol on it, and one of them is a perfect match for the symbol left at the scene of our colleague's murder.
We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract10_PactOfOberon.Contract10.Intro:
Esteemed colleague.
More information required regarding symbol present at colleague's murder scene.
Believe symbol appeared in certain painting.
Painting whereabouts unknown, but suspect it is in Elway's collection.
Possibly hidden with magic.
We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract10_PactOfOberon.Contract10.Description: Find a "certain painting," that can tell you more about the strange magical symbol you're investigating. The painting may be concealed using magic.
ST_Contract10_PactOfOberon.Contract10.Clue02:
Portman -
I understand parts of the house are in somewhat of disrepair at the moment, but there is a draft in the tower that I can't explain for the life of me. No obvious security issue, but I'm concerned for Miss Rowan's health, if I may be so bold.
- Dunfetter
ST_Contract10_PactOfOberon.Contract10.Clue01:
Portman- 
Miss Rowan gave me such a fright the other day. I was dusting the ground floor of the Tower and she appeared out of nowhere! I thought my heart would give out then and there!
- Margie
ST_Contract10_PactOfOberon.Contract10.Clue01_Facts: Rowan apparently has somewhere she can hide near the base of the Tower.
ST_Contract10_PactOfOberon.Contract10.Journal: The Guild wants you to gather more information regarding the magical symbol found near the slain Guildmember's body. They think the symbol may appear in a "certain painting," and that the painting might be in the Elway family's extensive art collection. The painting may also be concealed using magic. Go to the Elway Manor and learn what you can.
ST_Contract10_PactOfOberon.Contract10.QuestItem_Clue:
The Pact of Oberon (painting)
The painting portrays a Fey circled six magical stones. The Fey appears to be giving the stones to a group of humans. Each of the stones has a magic symbol on it, one of which is a match for the symbol found by the murdered thief.
ST_Contract10_PactOfOberon.Contract10.ContractFailure: You failed to leave with the painting.
ST_Contract10_PactOfOberon.Contract10.JournalComplete: You learned more about the magical symbol found near the dead Guildmember. You stole a painting called 'The Pact of Oberon" from the Elway Manor that depicts an ancient scene: the arrival of "Wardstones" in Kilcairn. The Wardstones were gifts from the Fey, and, in the painting, each one bore a magical symbol on it. One of those symbols was an exact match for the symbol found near the murdered thief. Tristan seemed to know more, but was reluctant to say much, except that you wouldn't like it when you learned the truth.
ST_Contract10_PactOfOberon.Contract10.ContractSuccess: You stole "The Pact of Oberon" painting.
ST_Contract10_PactOfOberon.Contract10.QuestItem_Clue_Facts: You've taken the Pact of Oberon

ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract11.QuestItem_Name_Clue:
Item of Interest File #ARC-00001
"Wardstones"
Gifted by Fey Prince Oberon as part of the so-called "Pact of Oberon." 
Pact details are unknown. 
Current number and whereabouts of the Wardstones are unknown.
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract11.QuestItem_Name_Clue_Facts:
"Wardstones"
Gifted by Fey Prince Oberon as part of the so-called "Pact of Oberon."
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract12_QuestItem_Clue:
Ancient Records
These documents appear to be in an ancient magical script, or possibly a code. Or both. 
(The Thieves' Guild may have the resources to decipher them.)
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract12.Title: Archival Archrival
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract11.Clue02:
Campbell - 
Chief Constable Avery has instructed that we are to restrict access to the archives. He has a list of sensitive files that he also wants relocated to the most secure part of the archive by the vault. See to it.
- Capt. Smith
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract11.Clue01:
Campbell - 
I know he's the Chief Constable, but running up to the archives on the second floor for him all the time is exhausting. And each request is more obscure than the last! What is a Wardstone? Damned if I know! I'm not a librarian, I'm a constable!
- Reynolds
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract12.Intro:
Esteemed colleague.
Sources say old deals exist between Elways and Fey.
Old family, many secrets.
Elway Manor best lead for information on Pact.
We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract11.Intro:
Esteemed colleague.
Suspect constabulary knows more. 
Search records for more information on Wardstones.
We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract11.Description: Find more information about Wardstones in the Constables' files.
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract11.Title: Historical Investigation
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract12.Description: Learn more about the Pact of Oberon by searching the ancient family records of the Elways.
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract12.Clue02:
Miss Elway, Mary, with your permission -
My man MacHuey has said there are several very old-looking documents left out in an archival area off one of the basement passages. I understand it doesn't fall directly under our security mandate, but he's concerned the documents may be damaged by all the water down there. If you need a pair of strong arms to help move your files, I am happy to assist.
Warmly, 
Capt. Dunfetter
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract12.Clue01:
Portman - 
The solicitor needs some of the older family records from the basement. I know the damp bothers your leg, but fetch them as soon as you can, please.
- Elspeth
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract12.Clue01_Fact: The Elways keep their older records in the basement
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract12.Journal: The Guild believes the Elways, one of Kilcairn's oldest families, have had dealings with the Fey and are the most likely source for more information regarding the Pact of Oberon. They have contracted with you to return to the Elway Manor and discover anything you can.
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract11.Journal: The Guild has contracted you to find more information regarding Wardstones in the files at the Constables' Guildhall. Hopefully this will shed more light on the strange painting you stole from the Elways.
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract11.Outro:
The Guildmistress is disappointed you weren't able to learn more from the constabulary, esteemed colleague, but never fear. We are researching new avenues that will help us unravel this conundrum.
We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract11.QuestItem_Name: The Pact of the Oberon Files
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract11.Clue02_Facts: The files are in the archives near the vault
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract11.Clue01_Facts: The files can be found in the archives on the second floor
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract12_QuestItem_Clue_Facts: The records have been collected but need to be deciphered
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract12.Outro:
This is the Guildmistress. Your successful retrieval of those documents has proven illuminating. As a reward, I offer you a peek behind the curtain.

Kilcairn was once an outlaw camp. Its ambitious leaders, including the Elway family, struck a pact with the Fey, Oberon. This pact secured six Fey Wardstones for Kilcairn's elite in exchange for the sacrifice of a thief every five years. The Wardstones' power transformed Kilcairn into a formidable, magically concealed city of the Old World, ruled by the Thieves' Guild's forebears. The magic of the Wardstones made whatever they protected, in this case Kilcairn itself, impossible to find.

Roughly 150 years ago, five of the Wardstones were broken, and the sixth vanished, causing Kilcairn's discovery by the modern world. The Guild was forced into the shadows and, with the Wardstones lost, we stopped the five-year tribute.

Unfortunately, Oberon is now determined to collect on this debt, jeopardizing us all. Mortal power alone cannot stop him. We need the final, missing Wardstone. With the Vistara Diamond, you possess the one tool capable of finding something that can't be found.

We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract12.JournalComplete: Upon deciphering the retrieved Elway records, the Guildmistress explained that ancient Kilcairn was  ruled by the Thieves' Guild's forebearers (including the Elways). These rulers  gained six Wardstones from Prince Oberon and used them to hide the city, promising the Fey a thief sacrifice every five years in return. The sacrifices ended 150 years ago when most of the Wardstones were destroyed and the last was lost. Now, Oberon demands his debt of lives, and the Guildmistress believes the final Wardstone is the Guild’s only shield. As the Wardstones make things impossible to find, your Vistara Diamond is the only tool that might be able to locate the missing Wardstone.
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract12.Clue02_Facts: Very old documents are kept in an "archival area" in the basement
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract12.ContractFailure: You failed to retrieve the information you were sent to collect.
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract11.JournalComplete: You found the constables' records regarding the Wardstones. They were apparently given as a gift as part of something called the Pact of Oberon, which served as the reference for the painting of the same name that you stole. The constables had no other details of either the Wardstones or the Pact itself, unfortunately.
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract11.ContractSuccess: You stole the Constables' information on Wardstones.
ST_Contract11_PaintingFiles.Contract11.ContractFailure: You were unable to retrieve the Constables' information on Wardstones.

ST_Contract13_OldMapPt1.Contract13.Title: A New Piece on the Board
ST_Contract13_OldMapPt1.Contract13.Journal: A mysterious individual reached out to you via the Electrogram to offer you an "off-the-books" Contract: retrieve an ancient map of Kilcairn that can help pinpoint the original location of the sixth Wardstone. The mysterious contact told you the map is within the archives of the Constables' Guildhall, so you'll start your search there.
ST_Contract13_OldMapPt1.Contract13.QuestItem_Clue:
Ancient Map
This piece of parchment clearly shows the original positions of the six Wardstones. Unfortunately, the map is so incomplete that it is impossible to connect the locations shown with any spot that would appear on a modern map. Maybe there are other pieces that would make the locations easier to find?
ST_Contract13_OldMapPt1.Contract13.Clue02:
Capt. Lindell - 
Per your inquiry, yes the archives contain a number of old maps of the city, some dating back centuries. All documents of that type are kept along the wall.
- Capt. Smith
ST_Contract13_OldMapPt1.Contract13.Intro:
Fancy device this. 
Bloody hard to write on, though.
I represent an interested party.
What do we want? 
To help you find the last Wardstone. 
Why? 
Let's just say we like the idea you would owe us a favor.
You'll need an old map of the city.
Check the Constables' Guildhall.
Cheers!
ST_Contract13_OldMapPt1.Contract13.Clue01:
MacDonald - 
I know the archives are restricted access now, but that doesn't mean you can just dump old documents on me to file! Get a clearance and do it yourself!
- Campbell
ST_Contract13_OldMapPt1.Contract13.Outro: Nothin' from the Guild. And nothin' from your anonymous benefactor either.
ST_Contract13_OldMapPt1.Contract13.Clue01_Facts: Old documents are kept in the archives
ST_Contract13_OldMapPt1.Contract13.Clue02_Facts: Old maps of the city can be found along a particular wall in the archives
ST_Contract13_OldMapPt1.Contract13.Dscription: Retrieve an ancient map of Kilcairn from somewhere in the Constables' Guildhall.
ST_Contract13_OldMapPt1.Contract13.QuestItem_Clue_Facts: This piece of parchment clearly shows the original positions of the six Wardstones but it's too old and worn to give enough information
ST_Contract13_OldMapPt1.Contract13.ContractSuccess: You found part of an ancient map.
ST_Contract13_OldMapPt1.Contract13.JournalComplete: You were able to retrieve a fragment of an ancient map of Kilcairn from the constables' archives. It was a little strange not to get a message from the Guild after completing the Contract, and your mysterious contact hasn't reached out yet either. The map fragment showed the original locations of all six Wardstones, but it wasn't possible to determine where those locations would correspond in modern Kilcairn. You're going to need a lead to more fragments of the map, if they exist.
ST_Contract13_OldMapPt1.Contract13.ContractFailure: You were unable to retrieve the ancient map.

ST_Contract14_OldMapPt2.Contract14.QuestItem_Clue:
Ancient Map
This piece of parchment is similar to the one you found in the Guildhall. With it, you can begin to see how the ancient city was laid out. With the third piece promised by your anonymous benefactor, you are certain you could find the Wardstone's original location.
ST_Contract14_OldMapPt2.Contract14.Outro: Doesn't look like your anonymous benefactor tried to make contact. I've been hearin' rumors the Kaiser might be lookin' for the Wardstone. Do you figure one of his spies might be the one feedin' us information?
ST_Contract14_OldMapPt2.Contract14.Description: Find a second piece of the ancient Kilcairn map kept by Mary Elway.
ST_Contract14_OldMapPt2.Contract14.Intro:
Heard you found the map. 
A piece, anyway.
Good on you. 
I have another.
You'll never guess who has the third.
Miss Mary Elway. 
With all three you've got a treasure map.
Once you have Elway's piece, we'll talk.
ST_Contract14_OldMapPt2.Contract14.Title: Mapping Things Out
ST_Contract14_OldMapPt2.Contract14.Clue02:
Mary - 
I found a strange scrap of parchment near a phonograph and immediately knew it must be yours. You should really put it with your other things before Rowan spirits it away.
- Elspeth
ST_Contract14_OldMapPt2.Contract14.Clue01:
Miss Mary -
I found an old bit of parchment and I haven't the foggiest where it should go. I placed it on a table at the base of the tower just so it won't get lost.
- Margie
ST_Contract14_OldMapPt2.Contract14.Clue01_Facts: The housekeeper put some parchment on a table at the base of the tower
ST_Contract14_OldMapPt2.Contract14.Clue02_Facts: There is a piece of parchment near a phonograph
ST_Contract14_OldMapPt2.Contract14.QuestItem_Clue_Facts: This piece of parchment is similar to the one you found in the Guildhall. With it, you can begin to see how the ancient city was laid out.
ST_Contract14_OldMapPt2.Contract14.ContractFailure: You failed to retrieve the second part of the ancient map.
ST_Contract14_OldMapPt2.Contract14.JournalComplete: You found a second fragment of the ancient map of Kilcairn, and you're certain that you can find the original Wardstone locations if you have a third. Unfortunately, your anonymous benefactor has not reached out. Tristan also told you there are rumors that the Kaiser's agents are hunting for the Wardstones. There's a chance one of those agents has been using you as a proxy. One way or another, you need that third map piece, and your anonymous benefactor claimed to have it.
ST_Contract14_OldMapPt2.Contract14.ContractSuccess: You stole another piece of the ancient map.
ST_Contract14_OldMapPt2.Contract14.Journal: Your "anonymous benefactor," as Tristan calls them, has informed you that they also have a fragment of the map you need. The third and final piece is apparently in the possession of Mary Elway, so you'll need to go to Elway Manor and find it.

ST_Contract15_ArrestReport.Contract15.Clue02_Facts: A transcription of the interrogation was taken outside the vault in the tower.
ST_Contract15_ArrestReport.Contract15.Clue01:
Capt. Lindell - 
I will be conducting the interrogation of the spy in the tower. Access is to be restricted.
- Chief Constable L. Avery
ST_Contract15_ArrestReport.Contract15.Outro:
Esteemed Colleague:
We should have suspected Montgomery sooner. No one is that stupid. If he had a map to the Wardstone, it is imperative to find it. That stone may be our only option for diverting Oberon's revenge.
We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract15_ArrestReport.Contract15.Intro:
Esteemed colleague.
Arrested German spy sought Wardstone.
Learn what he knew.
We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract15_ArrestReport.Contract15.JournalComplete: Hugh Montgomery, duped fiancé of Mary Elway, was actually a spy for the Kaiser. And he was your "anonymous benefactor" as well, it seems. Chief Constable Avery conducted his interrogation personally, and seemed keenly interested in the map's location. It seems the Chief Constable may know more about these affairs than you gave credit. You still don't have the third piece of the map, but the pieces are all starting to fit into place.
ST_Contract15_ArrestReport.Contract15.QuestItem_Clue:
Interrogation Transcript
Chief Constable Avery: You can drop the act, Montgomery. We know you're an agent of the Kaiser. We know what you were looking for. All we need from you is to tell us where you've hidden that map.
Montgomery: As I've told you over and over, I have no idea what you're talking about. I really must insist that I be allowed to speak to my solicitor.
ST_Contract15_ArrestReport.Contract15.Description: Learn what the Kaiser's spy knows about the Wardstone.
ST_Contract15_ArrestReport.Contract15.Clue02:
MacDonald - 
Chief Constable Avery wants me to transcribe the interrogation. They're using the secure area just outside the vault -- can you swtich wth me? I get nervous whenever the Chief Constable raises his voice.
- Campbell
ST_Contract15_ArrestReport.Contract15.QuestItem_Clue_Facts: The Chief Constable is also looking for pieces of the map
ST_Contract15_ArrestReport.Contract15.Journal: The Guild has reached out again. It seems one of the Kaiser's spies has been arrested and is being held at the Constables' Guildhall. Since the Kaiser is searching for the Wardstone, learning what this spy knows is of the utmost importance.
ST_Contract15_ArrestReport.Contract15.Title: The Kaiser's Man in Kilcairn
ST_Contract15_ArrestReport.Contract15.Clue01_Facts: The spy was interrogated up in the tower
ST_Contract15_ArrestReport.Contract15.ContractFailure: You failed to recover the information the Guild sought on the Kaiser's spy.
ST_Contract15_ArrestReport.Contract15.ContractSuccess: You recovered information regarding the Kaiser's agent for the Guild.

ST_Contract16_ValuableTrinket.Contract16.Clue02:
Capt. Lindell - 
More and more often lately, I go to deliver a report to the Chief Constable, I'm told he's in his office, and there's not a soul there when I arrive. Is he avoiding me?
- Capt. Smith
ST_Contract16_ValuableTrinket.Contract16.QuestItem_Clue01:
Chief Constable's Log Book
This log book contains a full accounting of the current Chief Constable's time in Kilcairn. It seems he is no mere police officer, but in fact is an agent of the British Crown. He is attempting to locate the Wardstone. Your name, and even some very blurry photos, appear frequently among the entries in the book. He knows about the Pact, the Kaiser's efforts in Kilcairn, the Tsar, and more.
ST_Contract16_ValuableTrinket.Contract16.Intro:
Esteemed colleague.
Chief Constable is the key.
Need to discover his secret.
We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract16_ValuableTrinket.Contract16.Description: Find out what the Chief Constable knows.
ST_Contract16_ValuableTrinket.Contract16.Clue01:
Ross - 
Regarding your complaint that Chief Constable Avery "scared you half to death" while you were doing a maintenace repair in his office last night: what exactly do you want me to do about it?
Capt. Lindell
ST_Contract16_ValuableTrinket.Contract16.QuestItem_Clue01_Facts: The Chief Constable is an agent of the British Crown and knows so much more than you expected.
ST_Contract16_ValuableTrinket.Contract16.Clue01_Facts: The Chief Constable somehow snuck up on a maintenance worker in his office
ST_Contract16_ValuableTrinket.Contract16.Title: The Chief Constable's Secret
ST_Contract16_ValuableTrinket.Contract16.Clue02_Facts: The Chief seemed to disappear from his office
ST_Contract16_ValuableTrinket.Contract16.Journal: The Guild believes the Chief Constable may hold the key to solving the Wardstone mystery. Perhaps he has the final map piece? Whatever the case, he knows something, and the only way to find out what is to break into the Constables' Guildhall one final time.
ST_Contract16_ValuableTrinket.Contract16.JournalComplete:
When you got into the Chief Constable's hidden office, you learned his secret: he is working directly for the Queen and his mission is to retrieve the Wardstone. This means Kaiser Wilhelm, Tsar Nicholas and Queen Victoria are all searching for it! Meanwhile, the Guild needs it in order to protect itself from the Fey and Oberon's murderous desire for thief sacrifices. You hold the only magical item that can actually locate the Wardstone if you can find the right area to search. How many people know you have it? And what might they do to get it?

TO BE CONTINUED...
ST_Contract16_ValuableTrinket.Contract16.ContractFailure: You failed to recover what you needed from the Chief Constable.
ST_Contract16_ValuableTrinket.Contract16.ContractSuccess: You stole the Chief Constable's notes.
ST_Contract16_ValuableTrinket.Contract16.Outro: You've really stepped in it, haven't you? Tsar Nicholas, Kaiser Wilhelm, and Queen Victoria are all trying to get their hands on this Wardstone. The Guildmistress needs it so no more thieves get themselves nicked and sacrificed. And here you are, with the one magical object that can actually find the bloody thing. What are you goin' to do now, eh?

ST_Contract3.Contract3.Outro:
Esteemed Colleague: 
The information you have gleaned regarding both Mary Elway and the Vistara Diamond has proved most illuminating! Whatever Elway's plans for the stone may have been, now that it is under the control of the Guild, we have ideas of our own how it might be best employed. 
We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract3.Contract3.Intro:
Esteemed colleague.
Looking into Mary Elway. What is her interest in Vistara Diamond?
Go to Elway Manor. Find Mary Elway's personal papers. See what she knows.
We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract3.Contract3.Description: Go to Elway Manor and find Mary Elway's personal papers. See what she knows.
ST_Contract3.Contract3.TItle: In the Eye of the Beholder
ST_Contract3.Contract3.Clue01:
Margie -
Please refrain from moving my personal papers when straightening my room. I have too much to do with wedding preparations to be spending time reordering documents!
- Mary
ST_Contract3.Contract3.Clue02:
Mary - 
I couldn't help noticing that you'd left some rather private papers out in your room. It wouldn't do for your beloved fiancé to find them, I sure you'll agree.
Your loving sister, 
Elspeth
ST_Contract3.Contract4.MaryElwaysPapersFACTS: Mary Elway has plans to restore her family's standing by using the Vistara Diamond.
ST_Contract3.Contract3.Clue02_Facts: Mary Elway has senstive information in her room.
ST_Contract3.Contract3.Clue01_Facts: Mary Elway is keeping her personal papers in her bedroom.
ST_Contract3.Contract3.Journal: Mary Elway, fiancé to Hugh Montgomery, requested the Vistara Diamond specifically as a condition for accepting Montgomery's marriage proposal. The Guild is curious to learn why and has contracted you to break into the Elway estate and learn what you can.
ST_Contract3.Contract3.MaryElwaysPapers:
Mary's Diary Entry
Montgomery is a hopeless dolt. He was only too happy to smuggle the diamond into Kilkairn for me. With its ability to render its holder impervious to magical illusions, I can finally put my plan into action. Restoring the Elway family's fortunes is no longer merely a dream! It pains me to have to wait while the diamond is displayed "in celebration of our nuptials." What farce! Nonetheless, it will be in my hands soon enough! I must be strong.
ST_Contract3.Contract3.ContractFailure: You didn't get Mary Elway's personal papers.
ST_Contract3.Contract3.JournalComplete:
You discovered Mary Elway's diary and now know more about both her and the Vistara Diamond. According to Elway, the diamond makes the holder completely resistant to magical illusions. Elway planned to use the gem to restore her family, though the exact nature of her plan remains unclear.

One thing is certain, however. Her engagement to Montgomery was a sham intended only to secure the diamond. Montgomery himself seems to have been nothing more than a useful idiot.
ST_Contract3.Contract3.ContractSuccess: You got Mary Elway's personal papers!

ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract5.LootObject_Note_Fact: A priceless Fabergé egg, was stolen from someone by the name of Father Gregory.
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract4.Clue01:
Angus - 
It's been three times this week I see a senior officer in the basement, salute him as he goes by, and then turn around and he's vanished. Always in that same alcove near the vault. As if the basement weren't spooky enough as it is!
Campbell
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract5.Clue01:
Bert - 
We'll be taking aperitifs in the courtyard with our guests. Please arrange for appetizers that won't attract the ravens.
-Mary
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract4.Clue02:
Capt. Lindell - 
The cleaning staff keep complaining about hearing "supernatural noises" near the vault in the basement. It's reached the point they're refusing to clean down there. 
- Ross.
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract5.Outro:
Esteemed Colleague:
As you may know, the Guildmistress does not ordinarily involve herself in disputes among members. However, in this case, the thief in question is operating without our sanction. As such we will provide information as we learn it.
We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract5.Intro:
Esteemed colleague.
Message received from "Father Gregory." Message follows:
"Will dine at the Elways tonight. Will leave instructions for you there."
Matter in your hands now.
We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract4.Intro:
Esteemed colleague:

Constables' Guildhall basement rumored to contain hidden rooms. Many colleagues lost trying to unveil them. 
Vistara Diamond changes everything.
Uncover constables' secrets. Succeed where others failed.

We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract5.JournalComplete: Father Gregory revealed himself to be a representative of Nicolas Romanov II, the exiled Russian Tsar. He wants to retrieve a Fabergé egg that was stolen and was presumably concerned that the Guild would not allow the Contract. The Guild, however, does not seem concerned, as Father Gregory's presumed thief operates without the Guild's blessing or protection.
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract5.Clue02_Facts: Father Gregory spent significant time in the courtyard.
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract5.Journal: Father Gregory wishes to hire you, but won't reveal specifics to the Guild. Instead, it seems he plans to leave you a note inside the Elway manor house with further details. This is surely a test of your abilities.
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract5.LootObject_Note:
Hidden Note from Father Gregory
My friend: 

I represent the exiled Tsar. When we were forced to flee Russia, we took with us such treasures as we could to keep them from being looted by the Bolsheviks. Imagine then our dismay when one of these treasures, a priceless Fabergé egg, was stolen from us. 

We wish to secure the egg's return, and would like to hire you for this purpose. Do you not say in English "set a thief to catch a thief"? I will await your favourable reply.

Father Gregory
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract4.Description: Investigate the lower levels of Constable Guildhall, making use of the Vistara Diamond to help see things that cannot usually be seen.
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract5.Clue02:
Miss Elspeth - 
In regards to Father Gregory, he seemed quite taken with the courtyard and spent quite some time there while I was giving him a tour of the estate.
- Portman
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract4.Journal: Now that you know more about the powers of the Vistara Diamond, the Guild is intrigued to see how it can be exploited. They want you to inflitrate the Guildhall and gain access to an area no thief has ever managed to breach.
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract4.Title: Secrets to Uncover
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract4.Clue01_Facts: Senior officers sometimes "disappear" in an alcove near the vault in the basement.
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract4.Clue02_Facts: Strange noises are sometimes heard near the vault in the basement.
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract5.Description: The Elways had a special dinner guest who left a message for you. Retrieve it.
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract5.Clue01_Facts: The Elways spent time with their guests in the courtyard.
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract5.Title: The Exiled Priest
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract4.LootObject_Note: This rolled piece of parchment appears to contain the elaborate ritual used by the police to create new "Hauntstables." Even skimming through it leaves you feeling slightly nauseated. It is apparently used as a punishment for corrupt Constables, consigning them to an eternity of vigilance after death.
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract4.Outro:
Well done, esteemed colleague.

Your discovery of the constabulary's secret ritual is most interesting. The Guildmistress is impressed. We've long wondered at the nature of the Hauntstables and their relationship to law enforcement of the more full-bodied persuasion.
Your efforts have been noticed beyond our circle as well. A certain "Father Gregory" has reached out to us in hopes of contracting with you specifically. He writes: "I'm impressed. I may have a discreet job for you. I will contact you through your guild."

We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract4.ContractFailure: You failed to get away with the Constables' secret.
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract4.ContractSuccess: You made off with the secrets to creating Hauntstables!
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract5.ContractSuccess: You retrieved Father Gregory's hidden note.
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract5.ContractFailure: You were unable to recover Father Gregory's note.
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract4.LootObject_Note_Fact: You've uncovered the secret of how Hauntstables are created, and demonstrated that the Vistara Diamond can find even magically hidden things.
ST_Contract4_HiddenSecrets.Contract4.JournalComplete: Your test of the Vistara Diamond allowed you to find the magical entrance to a secret room in the Guildhall! The room contained information on the ritual used to create Hauntstables. Moreover, your ability to penetrate the deepest secrets of the Constables has drawn interest from outside the Guild. A priest named Father Gregory has reached out to you through the Guild in hopes of hiring you.

ST_Contract6_NightingaleFile.Contract6.Clue02:
Campbell - 
I want the Nightingale file moved to the archives. We've had a number of documents go missing recently.
- Capt. Smith
ST_Contract6_NightingaleFile.Contract6.Clue01:
Capt. Lindell - 
I've gathered all the information we have on the Nightingale. I'm afraid there isn't much. As always, official files must remain on the second floor. You'll need to talk with Capt. Smith if you need an exception made.
- Sgt. Campbell
ST_Contract6_NightingaleFile.Contract6.Intro:
Esteemed colleague.
Thief you seek goes by "The Nightingale." Constabulary may have file.
We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract6_NightingaleFile.Contract6.Description: Find more information on the Nightingale by searching their files inside the Constables' Guildhall.
ST_Contract6_NightingaleFile.Contract6.Outro:
Greetings, Esteemed Colleague.
The Nightingale's list of acquisitions is impressive. If not for her independent status, we would be quite pleased. The high-profile nature of her thefts may have given her away, however. We will inquire if any of our contacts have heard tell of the missing items reappearing.
We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract6_NightingaleFile.Contract6.LootObject_NightingaleFile:
Person of Interest File #PER-89775
"The Nightingale"
Real name unknown.
Gender unknown.
Status active.
Associated thefts: Fabergé egg, The Eye of Argon, Prof. Martin's 1000 League Boots
ST_Contract6_NightingaleFile.Contract6.JournalComplete: The Constables' file on The Nightingale is almost laughable in its lack of detail, but it did at least contain a list of some of their recent acquisitions. The Guild will follow up and see if any of those items have re-appeared since they were stolen.
ST_Contract6_NightingaleFile.Contract6.Journal: The Guild has used their network to determine the identity, or at least the nom de guerre, of Father Gregory's thief: "The Nightingale." They have advised you to look for a file on The Nightingale at the Constables' Guildhall.
ST_Contract6_NightingaleFile.Contract6.Title: The Nightingale
ST_Contract6_NightingaleFile.Contract6.LootObject_NightingaleFile_Fac: The Nightingale is responsible for the thefts of a Fabergé egg, The Eye of Argon, and Prof. Martin's 1000 League Boots
ST_Contract6_NightingaleFile.Contract6.Clue02_Facts: The file can be found inside the archives
ST_Contract6_NightingaleFile.Contract6.Clue01_Facts: The file can be found on the second floor of the Guildhall
ST_Contract6_NightingaleFile.Contract6.ContractFailure: You failed to retrieve information about the Nightingale.
ST_Contract6_NightingaleFile.Contract6.ContractSuccess: You found the Nightingale's list of stolen items.

ST_Contract7_Retrieval.Contract7.Journal: At least one of the items stolen by The Nightingale seems to have been spotted at Elway Manor. This could hardly be a coincidence, could it? You'll need to investigate for yourself to learn the truth.
ST_Contract7_Retrieval.Contract7.Intro:
Esteemed colleague.
Word from sources is item on Nightingale theft list recently spotted at Elway estate.
Suggest investigating. 
We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract7_Retrieval.Contract7.QuestItem_Clue01:
Fabergé egg
This is certainly the same Fabergé egg stolen from Father Gregory by The Nightingale. Based on the evidence, The Nightingale would appear to be Rowan Elway.
ST_Contract7_Retrieval.Contract7.JournalComplete: Infiltrating the Elway estate finally allowed you to uncover The Nightingale's true identity: Rowan Elway, youngest of the three Elway sisters. The stolen egg has been returned to Father Gregory, and the Guild is extending an "offer" to Rowan to bring her into the Guild fold.
ST_Contract7_Retrieval.Contract7.Description: Inflitrate Elway Manor and recover the stolen goods. Use what you learn to unmask the mysterious Nightingale.
ST_Contract7_Retrieval.Contract7.Clue03:
Margie - 
Please stop moving my things. They may not look it, but the boots you took are a valuable magic item. I have taken them back up to the tower and I will thank you very much to leave them there.
- Rowan
ST_Contract7_Retrieval.Contract7.Clue02:
Miss Rowan -
I cam across a most unusual jewel in the tower. It may sound strange but it appeared to be a scarlet emerald. It looked quite valuable, so please inform me if you wish for me to put it somewhere safe.
- Portman
ST_Contract7_Retrieval.Contract7.Clue01:
Portman -
The strangest things keep showing up in the tower near where Miss Rowan likes to sleep up there. I don't know if I should tidy them or leave them be, and it's awful hard getting up there. 
- Margie
ST_Contract7_Retrieval.Contract7.Clue02_Facts: Portman, the butler, found an unlikely jewel in the tower.
ST_Contract7_Retrieval.Contract7.QuestItem_Clue03:
Prof. Martin's 1000 League Boots
Functional and tough-looking, these boots evoke a certain counter-cultural style. And they allow the wearer to travel vast distances with a single step! The constables' files say it was stolen by The Nightingale.
ST_Contract7_Retrieval.Contract7.QuestObject.Objective: Retrieve items stolen by The Nightingale
ST_Contract7_Retrieval.Contract7.Clue03_Facts: Rowan is storing a pair of magical boots up in the tower.
ST_Contract7_Retrieval.Contract7.QuestItem_Clue02:
The Eye of Argon
This jewel appears to be a scarlet emerald, which really doesn't make much sense. It does make it rare, however. It was on the list of items stolen by The Nightingale.
ST_Contract7_Retrieval.Contract7.Clue01_Facts: The housekeeper keeps noticing strange new items in the tower. Rowan Elway also uses part of the tower as a bedroom.
ST_Contract7_Retrieval.Contract7.Title: Unmasking
ST_Contract7_Retrieval.Contract7.Outro:
Unmasking the Nightingale is good work, esteemed colleague. The guildmistress is preparing a recruitment offer for her that we expect she will find impossible to refuse. Father Gregory's egg will be returned to him, per your contract. 
We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract7_Retrieval.Contract7.ContractFailure: You didn't recover what you needed to fulfill your Contract with Father Gregory.
ST_Contract7_Retrieval.Contract7.QuestItem_Clue03_Facts: You found a fashionable (and magical) pair of boots that were stolen by The Nightingale.
ST_Contract7_Retrieval.Contract7.QuestItem_Clue02_Facts: You found an unusual jewel that was on the list of items stolen by The Nightingale.
ST_Contract7_Retrieval.Contract7.QuestItem_Clue01_Facts: You found the Fabergé egg stolen from Father Gregory by The Nightingale.
ST_Contract7_Retrieval.Contract7.ContractSuccess: You identified the Nightingale and recovered Father Gregory's Fabergé egg.

ST_Contract8_SocialCalendar.Contract8.Title: A Wolf in Sheep's Clothing
ST_Contract8_SocialCalendar.Contract8.QuestItem_Clue:
Elway Social Calendar
Supper with former Tsar and aide.
Guest List: Nicholas Romanov II, Grigori Rasputin
ST_Contract8_SocialCalendar.Contract8.Outro:
Esteemed Colleague:
As the Guildmistress suspected, "Father Gregory" is, in fact, the famous and feared Rasputin, advisor to the former Tsar and noted magician. A most interesting personage and one we will be keeping our eyes on.
We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract8_SocialCalendar.Contract8.Intro:
Esteemed colleague.
Guildmistress requires verification of "Father Gregory's" true identity.
Suspect confirmation possible from Elways' social calendar. 
Ascertain who dined with them that night.
We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract8_SocialCalendar.Contract8.Description: Find the Elways' social calendar and identify "Father Gregory".
ST_Contract8_SocialCalendar.Contract8.Clue02:
Mary -
Rowan complained to me that your social calendar seems to dictate the pace of the entire estate. I told her if SHE had a social calendar of any sort we would take it into account, but she simply rolled her eyes at me. That girl!
Your loving sister, 
Elspeth
ST_Contract8_SocialCalendar.Contract8.Clue02_Facts: Mary Elway's social calendar seems to be predominate in what social events happen at Elway Manor.
ST_Contract8_SocialCalendar.Contract8.Clue01:
Miss Elway, Mary if I may be so bold -

If you would be so kind as to share your social calendar with me, it will help me improve security for the manor. 

Warmly, 
Capt Dunfetter
ST_Contract8_SocialCalendar.Contract8.Journal: The Guild is concerned about Father Gregory's true identity. They have contracted you to return to the Elway's manor and check the social calendar for the night Father Gregory dined there. It may provide details as to who he really is.
ST_Contract8_SocialCalendar.Contract8.JournalComplete: You confirmed that "Father Gregory" is, in fact, the magician Grigori Rasputin. Interesting on its own, but the involvment of such a powerful individual implies that something more may be afoot than just a stolen treasure.
ST_Contract8_SocialCalendar.Contract8.ContractFailure: You failed to leave with the social calendar you were sent to steal.
ST_Contract8_SocialCalendar.Contract8.ContractSuccess: You stole Mary Elway's social calendar and learned Father Gregory's true identity.

ST_Contract9_.Contract9.Clue02: Chief Constable Avery said he's going to look into the case personally. All material is to be transfered to his office.
ST_Contract9_.Contract9.Intro:
Esteemed colleague.
Missing colleague needs finding.
Guildmistress requests you.
Possibly held by constabulary. Suggest checking their guildhall.
We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract9_.Contract9.Description: Find the missing Guild thief, or evidence of his fate.
ST_Contract9_.Contract9.Clue02_Facts: Information on the missing thief can be found in the Chief Constable's Office
ST_Contract9_.Contract9.Clue01_Facts: Intel on the missing thief is on the second floor
ST_Contract9_.Contract9.QuestItem_Clue:
John Doe Murder Investigation
Subject: Unidentified male, found near a strange mystical symbol on a wall.
Foul play is suspected. The subject had thief's tools on his person.

From the Chief Constable: I don't care whether this man was a thief or the Prince of Wales. He's been murdered, and I want the case solved!
ST_Contract9_.Contract9.Title: Missing
ST_Contract9_.Contract9.Clue01: The Chief Constable has asked us to prioritize that unidentified person case, so we've moved the investigation materials to a secure location on the second floor.
ST_Contract9_.Contract9.Journal: The Guildmistress has requested you specifically for a contracted search for a missing Guild thief. The Guild believes the thief might be held at the Guildhall. If not, the constables may at least have leads to his whereabouts.
ST_Contract9_.Contract9.JournalComplete: The constables found a murder victim matching the description of the Guild thief who disappeared. According to the police report, the victim was found near an unidentified magical symbol. The Guild was not pleased with the news, and their reaction seemed to imply that they might know more about what happened than they were wliling to share with you.
ST_Contract9_.Contract9.ContractFailure: You failed to get what you needed to locate the missing thief.
ST_Contract9_.Contract9.ContractSuccess: You learned the fate of the missing thief.
ST_Contract9_.Contract9.Outro:
Your information is troubling, esteemed colleague.
We'll be in touch.

ST_Contract_Contract2_VistaraFiles.Contract2.Clue_04:
Capt. Lindell,

I'm concerned with how long Sgt. Campbell is taking to process the Vistara files. The Chief Constable has been breathing down everyone's neck to get them to him!

Sgt. MacDonald
ST_Contract_Contract2_VistaraFiles.Contract2.Clue_05:
Capt. Smith,

I'm hearing that your officers on the second floor aren't moving with adequate haste in getting the Vistara briefing assembled for the Chief Constable. Need I remind you this is a top priority right now.

Capt. Lindell
ST_Contract_Contract2_VistaraFiles.Contract2.Clue_03:
Capt. Smith,

Sgt. Campbell has been processing the Vistara files. If you need them, they're probably on his desk in the bullpen.

Sgt. MacDonald
ST_Contract_Contract2_VistaraFiles.Contract2.Clue_02:
Captain Smith,

All files related to the Vistara Diamond have been transferred to the Guildhall, second floor.

Sgt. MacDonald
ST_Contract_Contract2_VistaraFiles.Contract2.Clue_01: Chief Constable Avery wants everything we have on the Vistara Diamond immediately!
ST_Contract_Contract2_VistaraFiles.Contract2.Outro:
Congratulations on your successful pilfering of the Vistara Diamond files, esteemed colleague. 

We have been reviewing them and they are most interesting. It seems the Kilcairn constabulary keeps tabs on many objects of this sort. 
Regarding the diamond, Montgomery paid quite a tidy sum to smuggle it into Kilcairn. According to the files, Mary Elway, his bride-to-be, insisted on receiving it in return for accepting Montgomery's marriage proposal. 
The jewel has a long and twisting history. We will need to investigate further in order to best exploit this resource. 

We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract_Contract2_VistaraFiles.Contract2.Intro:
Esteemed colleague. 

Investigations into Vistara Diamond continues.
New information suggests police files on the jewel much more extensive than previously thought.
Infiltrate the Constables Guildhall, locate and retrieve the file.

We'll be in touch.
ST_Contract_Contract2_VistaraFiles.Contract2.Description: Infiltrate the Guildhall and steal files related to the Vistara Diamond in order to learn its history. Report back to the Guild upon success.
ST_Contract_Contract2_VistaraFiles.Contract2.Title: Into the Lion's Den
ST_Contract_Contract2_VistaraFiles.Contract2.Clue_ActualFiles:
Item of Interest File #ARC-17234
"Vistara Diamond"
Liberated from Calcutta by agents of His Majesty in 1873. Magical. Effects unknown.
Missing 1875. 
Sightings: 1899 - Amsterdam, 1907 - New York City, 1916 - London
Update: The diamond was recently brought to Kilkairn by Mr. Hugh Montgomery through irregular channels.
ST_Contract_Contract2_VistaraFiles.Contract2.Clue_01_Fact: The Chief Constable has taken a personal interest in the theft of the Vistara Diamond.
ST_Contract_Contract2_VistaraFiles.Contract2.JournalComplete:
The Constabulary's files on the Vistara Diamond were extensive, but still incomplete. What you now know for certain is that the gem has appeared intermittently around the world. It first came to the Constabulary's attention when it was looted from Calcutta about 50 years ago. The police seem to know even less than you do about the extents of the stone's power, however.

They do note that the gem was recently smuggled into Kilcairn by Hugh Montgomery, a wealthy industrial heir.
ST_Contract_Contract2_VistaraFiles.Contract2.Journal: The Guild wishes to learn more about the Vistara Diamond. As its current holder, you have been contracted to carry out the investigation. Your task is to retrieve certain files held at the Constables' Guildhall that can hopefully shed more light on the mysterious gemstone.
ST_Contract_Contract2_VistaraFiles.Contract2.Clue_ActualFiles_Facts: The Vistara Diamond was brought to Kilkairn by Mr. Hugh Montogomery, by irregular means.
ST_Contract_Contract2_VistaraFiles.Contract2.Clue_05_Fact: The Vistara files are being processed on the second floor.
ST_Contract_Contract2_VistaraFiles.Contract2.Clue_04_Fact: The files are on a constable's desk
ST_Contract_Contract2_VistaraFiles.Contract2.Clue_03_Fact: The files are on a desk in the bullpen
ST_Contract_Contract2_VistaraFiles.Contract2.Clue_02_Fact: The files are somewhere on the second floor
ST_Contract_Contract2_VistaraFiles.Contract2.Clue_06_Facts: The officer named Sgt. Campbell has a desk in the middle of the bullpen
ST_Contract_Contract2_VistaraFiles.Contract2.Clue_06:
To all concerned:
The bullpen reorganization is complete. We've had a comment from Sgt. Campbell that desks in the middle of the bullpen are subject to a great deal of noise. Please be mindful of your fellow officers who are doing important work.
ST_Contract_Contract2_VistaraFiles.Contract2.ContractFailure: You didn't get the Vistara Diamond files.
ST_Contract_Contract2_VistaraFiles.Contract2.ContractSuccess: You retrieved the Vistara Diamond files!

ST_Credits.Role.ChiefPeopleOfficer: Chief People Officer & Interim Operations Officer
ST_Credits.Role.Chameleon: The Chameleon / Insult Fairy / Talking Loot
ST_Credits.Game.Title: Thick As Thieves

ST_InGameUI.IntroCutscene.Line6: And now your journey through the shadows...
ST_InGameUI.IntroCutscene.Line2: It all happens here - new music, magic, science, ideas that are going to change everything.
ST_InGameUI.IntroCutscene.Line4: Kilcairn, city of the brightest lights…
ST_InGameUI.IntroCutscene.Line3: Money coming in from India, America, the Aztec Kingdoms.
ST_InGameUI.IntroCutscene.Line1: Welcome to Kilcairn. City like no other, at the dawn of the 20th century.

ST_Interactables.PromptCannotPickUpDeployableDueToAmmoLim: Not Enough Room to Pick Up

ST_Lockpicking.Locked.Vault.IncorrectKey: A vault key can only open the vault it was made for. This is the wrong vault for the key you have.
ST_Lockpicking.IncompleteMessage: You need to pick at least one of the sections. Try again?

ST_MapNames.OldManor.MapFlavorText: This is the estate of the three Elway sisters. Although it has fallen into disrepair, both the house and the family that holds it have a very long history in Kilcairn.

ST_MapTool.Guildhall.MapFlavorText: This is the headquarters and operational base for the Constables, Kilcairn's police force.

ST_MatchQuest_Facts_Abbessford.OldManor.Set.Briefing: Find all of the Claymur Urns in Elway Manor. They are only valuable as a set.
ST_MatchQuest_Facts_Abbessford.OldManor.Briefing: Find the correct vault and take what you came for!
ST_MatchQuest_Facts_Abbessford.Oldmanor.LootTheRoom: Steal as much as you can to meet the Guild's quota!
ST_MatchQuest_Facts_Abbessford.OldManor.TowerVault.Key1_0: The guard with the key is on the balcony of the main building.
ST_MatchQuest_Facts_Abbessford.OldManor.BasementVault.Key: The guard with the vault key is in the second floor banquet hall
ST_MatchQuest_Facts_Abbessford.OldManor.General.Hauntstables.Var1.FACT: The guards have been warned about playing records near the Hauntstables.
ST_MatchQuest_Facts_Abbessford.OldManor.General.Hauntstables.Var2.FACT: The maid accidentally incapacitated a Hauntstable by playing music near it.
ST_MatchQuest_Facts_Abbessford.OldManor.Vault: The quest loot is in the {VaultLocation}. You will need a key to access it
ST_MatchQuest_Facts_Abbessford.OldManor.GallaryVault.Key1: The safe in the lounge code is {Safe}. It has the vault key in it.
ST_MatchQuest_Facts_Abbessford.OldManor.GallaryVault.Key2: The safe outside in the courtyard code is {Safe2}. It has the vault key in it.
ST_MatchQuest_Facts_Abbessford.OldManor.BasementVault.Key2: There is a key in a hidden room. Toggle the ax on the base of the lion statue in the gallery to find it.
ST_MatchQuest_Facts_Abbessford.OldManor.TowerVault.Key1: There is a key in a hidden room. Toggle the bust on the bookshelf in Elspeth's bedroom to find it.

ST_Metagame.AnalyticsConsentPrompt: Help us improve Thick as Thieves by sharing gameplay data (gear picks, session length, level outcomes). You can turn this off in Settings anytime. For more information, please see our Privacy policy.

ST_NetworkErrors.OutdatedServer: The server is running an incompatible version of the game.
ST_NetworkErrors.OutdatedClient: The server you are trying to join is running an incompatible version of the game.  Please try upgrading your game version.

ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Basement.Clue04.Var2:
Capt - I thought the safest place for that key was the hidden room off the Gallery. So that's where I put it. Also, it's Harker's birthday today, so I've let him take a long lunch. 
- MacHuey
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Basement.Clue01.Var5:
Capt D - 
Yes, I think Mary wanted the {Item} locked in the Basement Vault for now. She asked me to reply to you, as she is busy with planning her upcoming wedding to Hugh Montgomery. 
- Miss Elway (Elspeth)
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Basement.Clue02.Var3:
Captain - 
Yes sir, I've got the key. If you need me I'll be near the banquet hall. Second floor. The other lads can't be trusted around food. 
- MacHuey
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Gallary.Clue03.Var1:
Captain Dunfetter - 
I put the vault key in the courtyard safe. If you should need to access it, the code is {Safe2}. Oh, and I just put in a request for transfer - this Elway place is altogether unnerving. 
- MacHuey
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Basement.Clue03.Var4:
Captain Dunfetter - 
In answer to your inquiry, there is a secret room near the Elway Gallery. To access it one must manipulate the ax on the nearby lion statue. It is therefore unlikely to be discovered accidentally by your typical burglar, should you decide to include it in your security measures. 
- H. Mistral, Solicitor
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Gallary.Clue03.Var2:
Captain sir  - 
Here's the code to that safe in the courtyard - {Safe2}. One of the Elway sisters asked me to put the vault key inside. So all secure on my end. 
- MacHuey
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Tower.Clue04.Var3:
Dunfetter - 
If you need the key, it's in a hidden room near my bedchamber. Portman can show you how to access it. 
I remain, 
Mary Elway
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Set.Item2.Var5:
Dunfetter - 
No, their man is too old to carry anything, gets winded just saying hello. So I put the {Item} in the {Location} myself. Everything is secure. 
- MacHuey
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Tower.Clue02.Var3:
Dunfetter - 
Yes, I've got the key. I'll be on the high balcony today should anyone need it. 
- MacHuey
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Gallary.Clue03.Var5:
Dunfetter - 
You mean the vault key? Yes, it's being held in the courtyard safe. I doubt you'll need it, however the combination is {Safe2}. Nothing else to report. 
- MacHuey
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Tower.Clue03.Var1:
Eleanora - 
My love, I've had the craftsmen devise a hidden room, and have commissioned a bust - which will be the clever way to access the room. Tell no one, it will be our secret. 
Our home will be our castle, 
Stephan
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Set.Item1.Var2:
Elspeth - 
Have Portman put those {Item} in the {Location}. Honestly, I feel like the place would fall apart without my constant vigilance. 
- M
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Tower.Clue01.Var5:
Elspeth - 
I'll need you to store the {Item} in the Tower Vault - it's as secure a place as any, and the trip up the stairs will do you good. I'd ask Margie, but she's far too prone to overexertion. 
Your sister, 
Rowan
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Gallary.Clue01.Var2:
Elspeth - 
I've had Portman move the {Item} into the Gallery Vault. I don't want it left out with all these guards around. Also, can you keep an eye on Rowan? I'm worried about her, but I'm busy with wedding plans. 
- M
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Basement.Clue02.Var4:
Elspeth - 
Tell Mary I've given the key to one of the guards, the fancy one in the second floor banquet hall. I'd tell her myself, but if I hear one more word about her betrothed I shall surely bethrottle her. 
Your sister, 
Rowan
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Gallary.Clue02.Var3:
Elspeth - 
The key in question is kept in the lounge area safe. I've recently changed the code, on the advice of Captain Dunfetter. The new code is {Safe}. 
Your sister, 
Mary
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Set.Briefing.Var1:
Esteemed Colleague - 
Find and retrieve the Claymur Urns. These priceless artifacts have long been in the Elway's private collection. A 45 minute window has been provided. Once you have them, a magic door will be made available for your escape. It will only stay open for 8 minutes. 
- We'll be in touch
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.Oldmanor.LootTheRoom.Briefing.Var1:
Esteemed Colleague - 
The Elway family has collected treasures for centuries. Your task is to unburden them of a few baubles. You have a window of 30 minutes, so efficiency is key. Once you have enough, a magic door will be made available for your escape. It will only stay open for 8 minutes. 
- We'll be in touch
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Briefing.Var1:
Esteemed Colleague - 
Your target, the {Item}, is held in a vault in the Elway Manor. A magic door will be available for 45 minutes to make your escape once the item is acquired. The door will last for 8 minutes once summoned.
We'll be in touch
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Set.Briefing.Var2: Esteemed Colleague - Find and retrieve the Claymur Urns. These priceless artifacts have long been in the Elway's private collection. A 45 minute window has been provided. Once you have them, a magic door will be made available for your escape. It will only stay open for 5 minutes. - We'll be in touch
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.LootTheRoom.Briefing.Var2: Esteemed Colleague - Steal anything you can possibly find from the Elways. Remind them that the Guild is always watching, and always able. A 30 minute window has been provided. Once you have enough, a magic door will be made available for your escape. It will only stay open for 5 minutes. - We'll be in touch
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.LootTheRoom.Briefing.Var4: Esteemed Colleague - The Elways have been stealing from our targets for quite some time, and we should reallocate their pilfered goods to their rightful owners: ourselves. It's no easy job, the Elway sisters just spent a fortune on security despite that manor in great need of repair. - We'll be in touch
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Set.Briefing.Var5: Esteemed Colleague - The Elways stole the Claymur Urns decades ago, and we have reason to believe they are still somewhere in that old manor. It's no easy job, the Elway sisters just spent a fortune on security. Seems they are protecting something. - We'll be in touch
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Briefing.Var5: Esteemed Colleague - Them Elways took the {Item} a few years back, and never did return it. Typical. Bunch of crooks, all of 'em. Anyway, boss wants it back, and it could be tricky. That mansion is a piece of work. - We'll be in touch
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Set.Briefing.Var3: Esteemed Colleague - We have learned that the Claymur Urns are being hidden at the Elway estate. You'll be relieving the Elways of these precious items. You should know that the mansion is well protected. - We'll be in touch
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Briefing.Var3: Esteemed Colleague - We have reason to believe the {Item} is somewhere in Elway Manor. We want you to steal it. But be warned: the Elway sisters have spent heavily on security. - We'll be in touch
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Briefing.Var2: Esteemed Colleague - You will proceed to the Elway Manor, and there you will find and procure the {Item} by any means necessary. This will be your only instruction. - We'll be in touch
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.General.Hauntstables.Var2:
Grigg - 
I don't know what I should do! I fancied a bit of music and turned on one of the gramophones. When one of those ghostly beasts came by, it had a conniption! Reacting to the music it seems! I just know I've broke it, and I don't want it coming out of my pay!
Margie
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Set.Item1.Var3:
Grigg - 
I don't know why someone put the {Item} in my kitchen, but I brought it to {Location}. I need to send out for more lard and oats. 
- Bert
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Tower.Clue03.Var5:
Grigg - 
I were dusting that bookcase in Miss Mary's bedroom, and didn't I just touch that stone bust, and next thing I know there's a hidden room! They'll be having me dust and scrub in there no doubt! There's always something! 
- Margie
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Gallary.Clue01.Var1:
Grigg - 
I've place the {Item} in the Gallery Vault as we discussed. And I'll have the solicitors come and appraise the art, it might be time to sell something. Wages might be late this week. 
Mary
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Set.Item3.Var4:
Hugh - 
Yes, darling, the solicitor thinks the {Item} may well be worth something. But doesn't advise selling just yet. So for now it remains safely in the {Location}. Will the weather permit a stroll I wonder? - 
Yours, 
Mary
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Basement.Clue03.Var3:
June 1803 
Herman - 
Have the architects install a statue, a bear or lion, whathaveyou, and make some part of it the means to unlocking a secret room, to be retrofitted in the grand gallery. I've seen such things in castles and such. 
- S. Elway
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Tower.Clue03.Var3:
Lord Elway - 
Enclosed are the plans for the hidden room you requested. Yes, we can place the trigger to access the room on the alabaster bust, or any other solid object on that nearby bookcase. Let us know your final thoughts, as construction will begin by midweek. 
-  CW
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.General.Hauntstables.Var1:
MacHuey -
Tell the men to stop playing the gramophone when Hauntstables are nearby! It disrupts them violently!
- Dunfetter
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Set.Item2.Var4:
MacHuey - 
As a favor to the sisters, would you move the {Item} to a more secure location before your shift starts? May I suggest the {Location}? That's a good man. 
- Dunfetter
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Basement.Clue04.Var4:
MacHuey - 
Be sure to place the key in the hidden room near the Gallery before you begin your shift. Report any issues or concerns. 
- Dunfetter
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Basement.Clue01.Var2:
MacHuey - 
Double check the Basement Vault, will you? The {Item} has been placed there for safekeeping. I'll want a report by evening. 
- Capt D
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Gallary.Clue02.Var2:
MacHuey - 
Extra double secret, so be sure to burn this after you read it. I've placed the key into the lounge area safe box. Here's the code should you need it: {Safe}. Carry on. 
- Dunfetter
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Gallary.Clue01.Var5:
MacHuey - 
I stand corrected. You were right. Have the {Item} moved to the Gallery Vault immediately. I'll update the manifest. Good work. 
- Dunfetter
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Basement.Clue04.Var1:
MacHuey - 
I'm leaving you in charge for the day. I've put the key in the hidden room near the Gallery. You remember how to access it? Good. Capt Dunfetter
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Basement.Clue03.Var5:
MacHuey - 
I've just found out how to access the hidden room. One needs to handle the ax that adorns the lion statue. That does the trick. I'd like to keep it all hush for now, until I know exactly the parameters of our assignment. 
- Capt D
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Set.Item2.Var2:
Margie -
If it's not to heavy for you, could you put the {Item} away? Miss Elspeth suggested the {Location}. I'll take my biscuits in the courtyard today, weather permitting. 
- Grigg
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Tower.Clue01.Var1:
Margie - 
I took all those dusty papers and {Item} and put everything in the Tower Vault. Young Miss Elway should know better than to leave such things laying about. I'll have my tea in the kitchen today, if you don't mind. 
- Portman
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Basement.Clue04.Var3:
Margie - 
I've put the key in the gallery hidden room. I'm sure one or other of the miss Elways will be asking for it before long. 
- Portman
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Tower.Clue03.Var4:
Margie - 
Please give the bust on the bookshelf a good dusting this week. It's been a bit slow to open the secret room, and I fear the mechanism has become soiled over time. And please tell Bert one less for dinner, as I'll be dining out with my fiancé. 
- Mary
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Basement.Clue01.Var1:
Margie - 
Put all those old papers and the {Item} into the Basement Vault this very moment. We need this place looking sharp for Miss Elspeth's return, and I hate anything dusty, as you know. I'll want my tea in the sitting room today. 
- Portman
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Gallary.Clue03.Var4:
Margie - 
Should you need to access the key, it's in the safe in the lounge. I believe the combination is {Safe2}. If that doesn't work, find Portman, he'll know. And have Bert do up a goose tomorrow, my young man will be joining us for the meal. 
- Mary
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Gallary.Clue01.Var4:
Margie - 
Tell the three of 'em that I moved the {Item} out of the hallway and into the Gallery Vault. And tell Portman too. I should be in the kitchen, not hauling things about. 
- Bert
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Tower.Clue01.Var4:
Mary - 
Did you send old Portman up to the Tower Vault to lock away the {Item}? I think the trip nearly did him in. I think next time send that young Captain who's so fond of you. Dumfeller? 
Your sister, 
Elspeth
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Set.Item1.Var1:
Mary - 
I put grandpa Stephan's things including the {Item} in the {Location} for now. I'm not sure we can trust these guards. But I defer to you on security. 
Your loving sister, 
Elspeth   

PS: Rowan took my hair brush. Would you talk with her?
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Tower.Clue04.Var4:
Mary - 
I returned the key to the hidden room near your quarters. On another subject, I'm not sure I didn't see our Margie conversing with one of the guards. I don't think I can weather a scandal just now. 
Your sister, 
Elspeth
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Basement.Clue04.Var5: Mary Darling - I see your predicament, but why not stash the key in the exemplary hidden room, the one by the Gallery? Seems a nice out of the way spot. - Hugh
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Set.Item3.Var2:
Mary, darling - 
I think the {Item} should be tucked away somewhere safe, like the {Location}. We're not yet married, but I do hope you can take my advice on this matter. 
Yours, 
Hugh
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Tower.Clue03.Var2:
Mary, my sweet - 
I await the day of our nuptials with great anticipation. The tour of your home was most gratifying. I am pleased the manor has a hidden room, if I remember correctly, accessed by moving a bust on the bookcase? Ingenious. I'm sure I will have much use of it. 
- Hugh
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Tower.Clue02.Var1:
Miss Elway, Mary, if I may - 
It's understandable you're worried about the Tower Vault, but rest assured, the only key is with a guard, my best man, MacHuey, he's the one on the high balcony duty. So as safe as can be! 
Fear not, 
Capt D
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Basement.Clue02.Var2:
Miss Elway, Mary, if I may be so bold  -  
I have left the key with one of my best men. He's in the banquet hall today, second floor. I hope that eases any worries you might have had. 
Yours warmly, 
Captain Dunfetter
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Tower.Clue04.Var5:
Miss Elway, Mary, if you allow - 
I have settled on the hidden room near your quarters to house the key for the time being. Any other location seems inconvenient and not as secure. I do hope you agree. 
Warmly, 
Capt Dunfetter
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Gallary.Clue02.Var4:
Miss Mary -
 Begging your pardon, but I put that key in the Lounge area safe. I'll just remind you that the combination to unlock it is {Safe}. Will you and your young man be staying for dinner? 
- Portman
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Set.Item1.Var4:
Miss Mary - 
I'm sorry to trouble you, but I thought you should know that I moved the {Item} to the {Location}. And I'll remind you that I have a half day on Tuesday.
Kindly, 
Margie
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Tower.Clue01.Var3:
Miss Mary - 
I've had Margie bring the {Item} up to the Tower Vault as you asked. Seems she near fainted from exertion. Should I send for a doctor? 
- Portman
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Tower.Clue02.Var4:
Miss Stillcrest, I mean, Margie - 
Please don't forget me today, as I long for tea and company on the high balcony. My duties include keeping a keen watch and being trusted with the only key. So you can see my prospects are good. 
- Claude MacHuey
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Gallary.Clue02.Var5:
Morning items for Grigg: 
Clean out ice box. 
Place key in Lounge are safe. 
Change the combination to {Safe}. 
Polish Hugh's boots. 
Send out for the weekly necessities, per Bert's menus. 
Could be more later. 
- Mary
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.LootTheRoom.Briefing.Var3: Most Esteemed Colleague - Our sources inform us that Elway Manor is full of treasures. Let's unburden them of anything you can get your hands on. We trust you can procure them despite the security measures the Elway sisters have introduced. We wish you luck. - We'll be in touch
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Set.Briefing.Var4: Most Esteemed Colleague - The precious Claymur Urns are your target. They are currently within the Elway Manor. We trust you can procure them despite the security measures the Elway sisters have introduced. We wish you luck. - We'll be in touch
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Briefing.Var4: Our Most Esteemed Colleague - We are watching. You have many promising qualities. Test your skills next at Elway Manor, the last known location for the {Item}, something we would very much like to claim as our own.  Do not fail us. - We will be in touch
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Basement.Clue03.Var2:
Portman -
Be a dear won't you and show that new captain how to access the gallery hidden room? I'm afraid he'll need a demonstration, as I've already told him twice it's the ax adornment on the lion statue. I'll be in town today, with my Hugh. 
- Mary E
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Tower.Clue01.Var2:
Portman - 
I took that {Item} and put in the Tower Vault like you wanted. Oh, me poor lungs. I'll never be right again. I'll be needing the rest of the day off. 
- Margie
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Gallary.Clue01.Var3:
Portman - 
I'm sure Miss Elspeth didn't mean to leave it about, so I put the {Item} in the Gallery Vault. Did I do right? 
- Margie
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Basement.Clue02.Var5:
Portman - 
If you need the key, it's with one of the guards. I think Dunfetter has the man working in the banquet hall today. I know, second floor, my apologies for the stairs. 
- Elspeth
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Tower.Clue04.Var1:
Portman, 
I found a key, and didn't know what to do with it. So I put it in that odd hidden room near Miss Mary's bedroom. I think it's safe there. 
- M
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Basement.Clue03.Var1:
Portman, 
I was dusting that horrible lion statue, you know, near the gallery, and didn't I just touch the ax on the base, and oh, well see for yourself. Some sort of secret room. These Elways are going to be the death of me. 
- Margie
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Set.Item2.Var1:
Portman, I put the {Item} out of the way in {Location}. How many things can one person dust? Also, cook says it's bacon and porridge again. 
- M
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Gallary.Clue03.Var3:
Rowan -
Indeed you were right, the vault key is now kept in the courtyard safe. But Mary has changed the entry code to {Safe2}. Does that help with your project? 
Your sister, 
Elspeth
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Set.Item1.Var5:
Rowan - 
Can you and Elspeth please bring the {Item} somewhere safe? Perhaps the {Location}? I would just do it myself, but I really must attend to some wedding details, the lace on my dress is completely unacceptable! -
 M
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Tower.Clue02.Var5:
Rowan - 
Could you tell Elspeth I've given the key to one of the guards? The one stationed on the tall balcony. I'd tell her myself, but I've got to run into town to meet with my betrothed. 
Your sister, 
Mary
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Tower.Clue04.Var2:
Rowan - 
I put that old key into the hidden room. I trust you remember how to access it. Also, if you took Elspeth's hair brush, please return it. You know how difficult her hair can be. 
- Mary
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Basement.Clue01.Var3:
Rowan - 
I'm not sure why you want to know, but yes, I've had the {Item} brought down to the Basement Vault for now. You didn't take my cheek color, did you? You know you're altogether too pale for that shade. 
- Mary
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Gallary.Clue02.Var1:
Rowan - 
If you're not doing anything today, could you at least put the vault key in the safe? The one in the lounge. You need these numbers: {Safe}. 
Your sister, 
Elspeth    
PS: Don't bother Mary, this wedding business has her in a mood.
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Set.Item2.Var3:
Rowan - 
If you're not too busy with your nervous pacing, might you please put the {Item} away? Perhaps the {Location}? I would do it myself, but for my upcoming wedding. 
- Mary
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Basement.Clue02.Var1:
Rowan - 
Well, since you are altogether too moody to lift a finger to help with any household tasks, I have taken it upon myself to  leave the key with one of the guards. He's patrolling the second floor, around the banquet hall. 
With love, your sister, 
Mary
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Set.Item3.Var5:
Rowan - 
You and Elspeth should know that the {Item} has been moved to the {Location} at the request of our solicitors. Creepy old Hellebore, as you know him. And please return my pocket comb if you took it - I don't find it funny in the least. 
- M
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Tower.Clue02.Var2:
Rowan - 
You were right, the key is with the one named MacHuey. They've been putting him on balcony duty lately. All these guards, yet are we really any safer? 
Your sister, 
Mary
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Set.Item3.Var1:
To The Estate of William Carlisle Elway, Mary Annalise Elway, Executrix:  
Yes, the {Item} is of some value, and until sold or divided among heirs we suggest it be stored safely in {Location}. Let us know if we can be of further assistance. 
- Hellebore Scott Mistral, Solicitor
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Vault.Basement.Clue01.Var4:
To: D. Mistral, Solicitor 
I'm writing to request that you immediately update the Basement Vault inventory list to include the {Item}, which I have moved there just this morning in hopes of securing it against theft and damage. 
With thanks, 
M. Elway
ST_OldManor_BasementVault_Clues.OldManor.Set.Item3.Var3:
To: Miss M. A. Elway - 
Our appraiser believes the {Item} to be of substantial monetary and historical value. Therefore, we advise it remain safely in the {Location} until such time as you wish to sell, transfer, or donate. We hope to be of continued service. 
Truthfully, 
HSM, Solicitor

ST_Tools.CuckooClock_Tool_Description: A loud and obnoxious fairy glamour distilled into a magical tool. Grown men haven been brought to tears or thrown into a rage by its barbed tongue.
ST_Tools.Glamour_Tool_Description: A mask that copies the appearance and move speed of enemies. Not being stealthy or being observed by guards degrades the duration of the disguise. Security devices aren't fooled by the magic.
ST_Tools.VistaraDiamond_Tool_Description: A mysterious gem that allows the holder to see traps and treasures.
ST_Tools.Zipwire_Tool_Description: A spring-loaded grapple gun that launches out, connects to a surface and then quickly recoils its cable, yanking both the gun and its wielder along for the ride.
ST_Tools.SmokeBomb_Tool_Description: A throwable device filled with volatile chemicals that creates thick, oily smoke that obscures vision while the cloud of smoke is active. May also cause choking.
ST_Tools.Loadout.Slot.Outfit.Head: Head
ST_Tools.PIckpocket_Tool_Description: People tend to refer to any helpful magical tool as a "fairy," although this one resembles a fae more than some. Not strictly for picking pockets, it can also flip switches at a distance.
ST_Tools.MagicSlime_Tool_Description: The origins of the viscous goo known as "slither sap" are steeped in controversy. Some claim it was developed as a machine lubricant, others say a desert topping. Whatever the truth, it is extremely slippery. The fluid also has a strange interaction with ghosts when the two come in contact.
