ST_Tutorial.Tut_Smoke_Bomb_Blocks_LOS: A smoke bomb stops guards, Magic Eyes an' anythin' else from seein' ye.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Clue_Get_Key_to_Leave:
Aspiring Colleague: 
We are contracting you to acquire the Vistara Diamond. We've provided a key to unlock this safehouse. We'll be in touch.
- The Guild
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Objective_Collect_Major_Loot: Collect the valuable inside the chest
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Crank_Zipwire: Crank your Zipwire so it's ready to use it again. Hold to crank the tool until it finishes.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Clue_02_Engagement:
El, 

I've heard. The missus can't stop talking about Hugh Montgomery and how bloody handsome and bloody rich he is. Sounds like he bought the rock for Miss Mary Elway as an engagement present. If I was you, I'd keep away from that diamond. Elways are an old Kilcairn family. They might be down and out, but they aren't ones to mess with.

- Sal
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Equip_Smoke_Bomb: Equip the Smoke Bomb with
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Objective_Enter_First_Alleyway: Exit the Building and Keep Exploring
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Quest_Objective_Generic_Name: Get in with the Thieves' Guild
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Objective_Get_Past_Magic_Eye: Get past The Magic Eye with smoke bomb
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Fey_Find_Key: Grab that key and let's be on our way.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Fey_Intro: Heard ye want tae join the Thieves' Guild. Name's Tristan. The Guildmistress wants ye tae fulfill a Contract tae see if ye make the cut. Steal some fancy diamond. C'mon over here an' we'll get started.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Fey_Find_Smoke_Bomb: Here's another chest. Bound tae be somethin' useful in there.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Fey_Take_down_infographic: If ye crouch down so yer quiet, ye can sneak from behind a guard and take him down quiet like. (Interstitial Next).
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Objective_Collect_Egg: Infiltrate the Jewelry Shop and Steal the Vistara Diamond
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Info_Keys: Keep track of yer keys. Differnt keys'll open different doors.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Use_Lean: Lean Left with Lean Right with
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Learn_Lockpicking: Locks are no problem for a real thief. Just takes a steady hand and good timing. (An interstitual about lockpicking will go here)
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Info_Ammo_Refills: Most tools use ammo. If you run out, look for one of these boxes to reload.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Movement_Intro: Move with Look with
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Use_Major_Loot: Nothin' says ye can't pilfer a bit extra while yer workin' fer the Guild, eh? Like from this chest, fer example.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Info_Stash_Intro: O' course YE'D never get caught, but since ye'll drop all yer loot if yer knocked out, why don't ye use that Stash? It's a magical tunnel that'll send yer loot back tae yer hideout.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Clue_03_Guard_Intel:
Only one guard is on patrol tonight. Harold's called in sick again.

Harold sucks eggs.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Use_Tool_Open_Map: Open the map by pressing
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Info_Take_Downs: Remember tae stay out of sight an' low tae the ground.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Clue_01_Shop_Info:
Sal,

You keen on what's up with the jewelry shop? They got some legendary diamond called the Vistara. They helped import it, so I guess they get to show it off for a bit. I bet it's worth a pretty penny. If Nick wasn't in the clink, it'd make a nice score.

- El
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Fey_Find_Map: Since yer new in town, yer goin' tae need a map. Ye can log that Clue ye found there as well!
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Use_Tool_Map_Change_View: Speakin' o' clues, use to check any clues you already read.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Use_Stealth_And_Shadows: Stick tae the shadows and ye'll be invisible tae guards even if they're close!
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Use_Mantle: Tap while moving into an obstacle to mantle over it
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Clue_Get_Key_to_Leave_FACT: The Thieves' Guild wants you to steal the Vistara Diamond as an initiation test.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Clue_01_Shop_Info_FACT: The Vistara Diamond is being displayed at a nearby jewelry shop.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Info_Zipwire: The Zipwire is a useful tool for moving quickly and reaching higher ground, but it does have limited range. Look for the reticule to turn white when a target is close enough. Push forward while zipping to climb over ledges.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Clue_02_Engagement_FACT: The diamond was purchased as an engagement gift by Hugh Montomgery for Mary Elway.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Clue_03_Guard_intel_FACT: The jewelry shop only has one guard on duty.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Use_Tool_Map_Close: The map can be closed again with
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Use_Tool_Map_Intro: The map lets you see where you are as well as showing other important locations if you have discovered the right clues. To review your clues, use to check the other page of the map.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Enter_Jewelry_Shop: There's the Jewelry Shop. Use what I've taught ye and ye'll have that diamond before ye know it.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_HUD_Clue_on_Compass: This is your Compass. It will indicate the direction of certain items in the world when you get close. Right now, it is indicating there is a clue nearby.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Use_Tool_Map_Clues_Page: This page shows all the clues you have collected. Check it to refresh your memory. The map can be closed again with
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Objective_Collect_Smoke_Bomb: Unlock the chest and collect the item inside
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Objective_Lockpick: Unlock the chest and collect what's inside
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Use_Crouch: Use to move silently and get through tight spaces.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Objective_Leave_the_Building: Use the key to unlock the door
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Objective_Use_Stash: Use the thin location to stash your valuables
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Contract1_Outro: Welcome to the Thieves' Guild, esteemed colleague. Stealing the Vistara Diamond would be a feather in a thief's cap under any circumstances, but it appears this is no ordinary jewel. It's too hot to fence right now in any case, so hold on to it. We'll be in touch.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Fey_Find_Clue: When yer on a mission, keep an eye out fer useful intel. Clues'll show on yer compass if they're close.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Info_Stashes:
Ye  can only use a Stash once, so don't just go usin' 'em willy-nilly. Think first. And ye can't stash anythin' critical tae yer mission.

Alright, enough a' that. Follow me.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Info_Exit_Area:
Ye got it! 
Wait-- is the gem MAGICAL? Ye've a look in yer eyes like yer seein' things. Och. There's no time tae faff about now. We’ll hafta get it sorted later. Let's get tae the exit.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Info_Throwables: Ye've got the tools ye bring along on a contract, but sometimes ye'll need tae improvise. Throwin' a bottle will make a loud sound tae distract guards.
ST_Tutorial.Tut_Contract1_Journal: You successfully joined the Thieves' Guild! And managed to get your hands on a strange artifact as well. The "Vistara Diamond" -- the item you stole to prove yourself to the Guild -- seems to have the power to see things: guards, treasure, and perhaps more, but the full extent of its powers remain as shrouded in mystery as its origins.

ST_Tutorial_Infographics.Tut_Info_Smoke_Bomb_LOS: A smoke bomb stops guards, Magic Eyes, and turrets from seeing you if the smoke is between you and them.
ST_Tutorial_Infographics.Tut_Info_VistaraDiamond: As soon as you picked up  the Vistara Diamond, you equipped it. While it is equipped, it allows you to see things you normally wouldn't see, like the location of nearby guards, loot, and some security devices.
ST_Tutorial_Infographics.Tut_Info_Stealth_Orb_Intro: Darkness is a thief's best friend. When you're in total darkness, you are almost invisible to guards and turrets, even at close range. Your <Highlight>Stealth Orb</> will tell you how deep in the shadows you are.
ST_Tutorial_Infographics.Tut_Info_Guard_Alert_Continued:
Don't panic if you are spotted. Try to break line of sight and then find a good hiding spot.

You can use tools or the environment to break line of sight. The guards will return to their patrols if they lose you.
ST_Tutorial_Infographics.Tut_Info_Take_Down: If you crouch down and approach from behind, you can sneak up on a guard and take him down quietly. Some guards carry keys you can pickpocket, too.
ST_Tutorial_Infographics.Tut_Info_Lightswitches: Light switches can be toggled <Highlight>On</> and <Highlight>Off</> . Darkness is your ally, so shut the lights off whenever you can.
ST_Tutorial_Infographics.Tut_Info_Lockpicking: Locks require a steady hand. Use when your pick is over a highlighted section to work the tumbler. Locks are booby trapped, so expect a shock if you make a mistake.
ST_Tutorial_Infographics.Tut_Info_Ammo_Refills: Most tools use ammo. If you run out, look for one of these boxes in order to reload.
ST_Tutorial_Infographics.Tut_Info_Player_Speed_Final_Reminder: Remember, your movement speed directly effects how much noise you make.
ST_Tutorial_Infographics.Tut_Info_Player_Speed_3: Sprinting will make <Danger>A LOT</> of noise that could draw attention your way. Be mindful of how you move through the shadows.
ST_Tutorial_Infographics.Tut_Info_Stealth_Orb_Continued: Standing in direct light makes the orb glow <Highlight>white</>. If the orb is glowing, guards can see you -- even at a distance. As you move into shadows, the orb will darken and you will be harder to see.
ST_Tutorial_Infographics.Tut_Info_Stashes: Stashes are a great way to secure your loot during a mission, since you'll keep any cash you stashed even if you fail the mission! Each stash can only be used once and Mission-critical items will NOT be stashed.
ST_Tutorial_Infographics.Tut_Info_Stealth_Orb_Continued_02:
The Stealth Orb also shows you how much noise you are making. The <Highlight>louder</> you are, the more attention you will attract. <Highlight>Crouched walking</> is the only type of movement that is always silent.

Remember that <Danger>noise</> attracts guards, even if you are concealed in the shadows!
ST_Tutorial_Infographics.Tut_Info_Zipwire: The Zipwire is a useful tool for moving quickly and reaching higher ground. Look for the reticle to turn into a crosshair when a target is close enough. Move forward while zipping to mantle up. The Zipwire must be cranked after each use to ready it. Hold to crank the tool until it finishes.
ST_Tutorial_Infographics.Tut_Info_Security_Turret: Turrets have switches on them. Stay out of their field of view and use to turn them off (or on). Use Lean to help check where this turret is looking while staying out of sight behind the crates. Lean Left with Lean Right with
ST_Tutorial_Infographics.Tut_Info_Guard_Alert:
When a guard spots you, they'll get <Danger>suspicious</>. Suspicious guards <Danger>investigate</> the last spot they saw or heard something that got their attention. If they see you long enough, they'll <Danger>identify you</> and attack. <Highlight>The closer a guard is, the faster they will identify you.</>

Even if you are in darkness, if you make noise, the guards can find you!
ST_Tutorial_Infographics.Tut_Info_Summon_Exit: When you reach the exit you will need to stand in the marked area for a short time before you <Highlight>escape</> to your hideout.
ST_Tutorial_Infographics.Tut_Info_Throwable_Bottles: You can pick up bottles you find. Throwing a bottle will make a loud sound that can be used to distract guards. Keep in mind though, you can only hold one bottle at a time.
ST_Tutorial_Infographics.Tut_Info_Player_Speed: Your speed and posture are important to keep in mind when traveling through Kilcairn. Crouching creates no sound.

ST_Tutorial_ThievesDen.Tut_TD_Move_to_WantedPosters: Alright. Come over here next. See anyone ye know?
ST_Tutorial_ThievesDen.Tut_TD_Move_to_Mirror: C'mon over tae this Wardrobe next.
ST_Tutorial_ThievesDen.Tut_TD_Electrogram: Hear that ringin'? That'll be the Guild callin' on the Electrogram. Better see what they want.
ST_Tutorial_ThievesDen.Tut_TD_Contract_Intro: Looks like there's a note for ye.
ST_Tutorial_ThievesDen.Tut_TD_MapIntro: So ye've got your next Contract. Come on over tae the map table. Pick the Constables' Guildhall buildin' on the map. There's a Mission ye'll have tae do while ye're there, and ye can also work on your Contract. Just make sure to check if you're joining a co-op or solo experience before starting.
ST_Tutorial_ThievesDen.Tut_TD_Ledger_Contracts: The Journal is your personal record of your journey as a thief, tracking your progress and past Contracts as well as any active one. Next, look at the Black Market
ST_Tutorial_ThievesDen.Tut_TD_Mirror_Loadout: The Wardrobe will allow you to customize your appearance and also swap to different Thief's Tools that you have unlocked and acquired.
ST_Tutorial_ThievesDen.Tut_TD_WantedPosters_Intro: This is how you look for friends who are online and can team up with you. Unlike when you are matchmade with strangers, when you team up with friends you can continue to work on Contracts. The Contract of the player with the least story progress will be selected.
ST_Tutorial_ThievesDen.Tut_TD_Ledger_BlackMarket_01:
This is the Black Market, where you can buy everything! 
Speaking of, let's go ahead and purchase your <Highlight>Zipwire</>, <Highlight>Smoke Bomb</>, and <Highlight>Constables' Guildhall</> so you can get back to thievin'.
ST_Tutorial_ThievesDen.Tut_TD_Intro: Welcome tae your Thief's Den. Ye can relax here, change yer clothes, an' switch yer tools. The Guild'll also call ye on that Electrogram with any Contracts they want ye tae do.
ST_Tutorial_ThievesDen.Tut_TD_Ledger_Intro: Ye'll be needin' some things before ye take yer next Contract. Come over tae the Ledger.

Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Primary02: "STILL WORKS A HUNDRED YEARS LATER", claims Chief Inspector Windsmire. The sconce, after receiving a good tug, opens a nearby chest. All enjoyed a good laugh.
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.VariationA00:
All staff: 
The security device on/off switch has been relocated to the hallway in front of the vault. Please update your rounds accordingly. 
- Administrator's Office
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Vault10: Arresting officers caught a whiff of the talent on that statue. We handed it over to the Archivist, and good riddance.
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Vault5: Arresting officers caught a whiff of the talent on that statue. We handed it over to the Archivist, and good riddance.
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Chambers.Primary01: Attention! The Warburg Effigy will be kept in the Chambers Vault until further notice. - Commissioner's Office

Barnaby - 
Lester has the key again. Yes, the one who won your dinner ticket at poker last week. 
Worthington
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Chambers.Primary7:
Brimsby - 
I've had that strange statue placed in the Chambers Vault for the night. Please inform the patrols. 
- Shelly
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.Vault004:
Brimsby - 
Why bother moving the Effigy to the Tower Vault? Thieves don't just break-in from ground-level, you know 
- G
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Chambers.Primary01:
Brimsby - 
Yes, Lester has the vault key tonight. How he can eat after listening to that Effigy mutter to itself, I don't know. 
- Shelly
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Primary15:
Brimsby - 
Yes, in an emergency you can shut down the vault security measures using the switch on the back wall in the office. Maybe we should send a department-wide memorandum. 
-Shelly
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.VariationB00:
Brisby - 
The idea of moving the safe was to hide it. By scratching the floor, you've left quite a clue to the location, yes? Call the carpenters, you dolt! 
- M

C - 
So though we've ruined the floors dragging it about, I hear the only item in that new safe is a vault key. Is this a good idea?
- Smythe
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location004:
Cappy - 
After me sweep up, I come across that {Item}. I weren't gonna take it. I put it in the {Location}. 
- Grayson
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Chambers.Primary01:
Cappy - 
I got the vault key again tonight. Catch me at dinner. 
- Lester
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location016:
Cappy - 
I was mopping up and found the {Item}. Moved it to the {Location}. Gave it a good shine too. 
- Grayson
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Primary06:
Cappy - 
That statue's a plaster copy, is my bet. You can watch it all you want, I'll be in the lounge. 
- Albert
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location006:
Cappy sir - 
Someone left the {Item} in the wash-up. Seems like a nifty tidbit to me, so I moved it to the {Location}. Just for safekeeping. Hope I did right. 
- Constable Higgins
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location010:
Captain - 
I've taken it upon myself to move the {Item} to the {Location}, as this seems a more secure spot. I trust you'll agree. 
- Margery
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location027:
Captain Seawell - 
Here are your orders: 
Place the {Item} in safekeeping near the {Location}. 
Also, have the foyer swept, one should have pride in their surroundings. 
- Chief Constable L. A.
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location026:
Captain sir - 
Begging your pardon, but the {Item} is currently in the {Location}, because the cleaning staff kept finding it. Mind you, it's been polished up quite nice, so I'm not complaining. 
- MacKara
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location020:
Chief Constable - 
Per your orders, we've stowed the {Item} in your location of choice, the {Location}. Awaiting further instructions from you. 
- Higgins
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location021:
Chief Constable Avery, 
The {Item} is currently in the {Location}, if you'd care to inspect it. Furthermore, Tuesday you can tour the barracks and jail cells. Will this be satisfactory? 
- Seawell
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Chambers.Primary:
Clark - 
It's been moved to the Chambers for safekeeping. Nothing that magical should be kept above ground. 
-Robinson
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.VariationB00:
Clark - 
The Sergeant says he's docking my pay till the floors are refinished, but it was your idea to drag that safe!
 - Wallace.
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Vault7: Clark - stop talking with the Effigy in the Archives Vault. I know you don't have many friends, but it's frightening the staff. - Robinson
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Primary12:
Commissioner: 
The Warburg object's with Magical Affairs now, as I predicted. The Chief Constable will be keeping the key. 
- D. Ramstable, Customs
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.VariationA01:
Cook - 
Bring a sandwich to the vault's key-bearer in the basement. The sergent forgets he's down there, and he's looking a bit pale. 
- Martha
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Chambers.Primary01:
Crabapple - 
Yes, the horse painting will open a safe nearby. No, I didn't set it up. Just leave it alone is my advice. 
- Smythe
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Vault8: Crabapple - you can stop calling in sick now, you coward - they had us put the Warburg Effigy in the Archives vault. - Smythe
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location018:
Dear Captain Seawell - 
Once again I've stumbled over the hiding place of your precious {Item}. Purely by accident I assure you. However I did place it in the {Location}, as this seems a more sensible spot for it. 
- With admiration, Margery
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location032: Do what you want, if {Item}  is going to be in {Location} I'm not going anywhere near it until Magical Affairs vets it. I've seen things you haven't.
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Chambers.Primary01:
Doversham - 
That horse painting in the office? Don't touch it, don't even look at it. Odd things happen in there. 
- Verity
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location003:
Duffy - 
Careful, the captain noticed your whiskey breath. I think that's why he moved the {Item} to {Location}. Anyway, thought you should know. 
- J
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Briefing:
Esteemed Colleague -
Steal the magical artifact known as the Warburg Effigy. The Constabulary are keeping it in one of their vaults. Once you have it, we will provide a magic door for your escape. 45 minutes. Don't be late. The door will only last for 8 minutes once summoned.
- We'll be in touch.
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Briefing:
Esteemed Colleague - 
Find the set of gems needed to complete the Staff of Edinburgh. They're scattered among the trinkets in the Constables' Guildhall. A magic door will be available for your escape route for 45 minutes. Once it appears, it will remain for 8 minutes before collapsing.
We'll be in touch.
Toasts.Guildhall.LootTheRoom.Briefing:
Esteemed Colleague - 
The Constables' Guildhall is a repository of countless treasures seized over the years. Steal anything you come across and put them to better use than gathering dust in a collection case. There is only a 30 minute window for the job. Once you collect enough, a magic door will be summoned for 8 minutes before collapsing.
We'll be in touch.
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Chambers.Primary5: Evening Notables: The impounded property is in the Chambers. It's only one night, but we're not taking chances.
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.VariationA00: For those who have trouble with their letters - with the security device switch in tfront of the tower vault 'on' is *up,* and 'off' is *down.*   - Sergeant H.
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.VariationB00:
G -
They're ruining the floors dragging that safe, but all that's in it is the key to the vault.  Just give me the damn thing! 
- M
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.Vault005:
G - 
I'm a bit shaken. When we moved that awful Effigy to the Tower Vault, the wind up there sure sounded like ghosts.
T
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Primary03: G - Give the library sconce a pull, the chest opens right up. Thieves will never guess.
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Vault6: G - I'm calling in that favor. I'm not doing an Archive shift with that the Effigy chattering away. - W
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurment.Fact.Health: Health packs can be found around bathrooms
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location028:
Higgins - 
Change of orders. Now he wants the {Item} placed in the {Location}. Bit of confusion there, but it's sorted now. 
- M
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location017:
Higgins - 
For pity's sake, make sure the {Item} is well hidden in the {Location}. I'm worried it'll be damaged or stolen. Do you understand? 
- Chief Constable Avery
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location007:
Higgins - 
Someone tell Grayson to leave the {Item} in the {Location}. It's safest there, and he keeps moving it during his mop-ups. 
- D
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location013:
Higgins - 
Tell Grayson to avoid cleaning near the {Location} for the next few days. I've placed the {Item} there, and I don't want it disturbed. Also, I'm still waiting on those budget estimates for the coming year. 
- Seawell
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location019:
Higgins - 
There's a new Chief Constable, Logan Avery. He's the tidy type, if you know what I mean. He wants the {Item} kept in the {Location}. I suggest you don't delay. 
- S
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Primary13:
Highsmith - 
That spare key is in the Chief Constable's office tonight. Guess I shouldn't have touched that thing. 
- Nigel
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.Vault001: Horst - I heard you wheezing on the Tower stairs when we moved the Effigy up there. It's not enough to be clever - you've got to stay fit!
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Primary04: How does Reynolds open that thing up? All I see is a hallway and lighting fixtures. But I'll die before asking him. Fancy elitist bastard.
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.VariationB00: IF WE PUT THE VAULT KEY IN THE SAFE TO KEEP IT SAFE, SHOULDN'T WE PUT THE SAFE IN THE VAULT TOO? HORST
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Primary16: Important Safety Notification - In case of emergency the shut off switch to the vault security measures is located in the Chief Constable's office.
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Vault2: Inspector: Warburg Effigy stays in the Archives until I clear it of any magic. Read the rulebook.
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location033: Is {Item}  still in {Location}? Chief wants to know. Watch yourself, he's poking his nose in everywhere. Thinks he's still in London.
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location030:
J- 
Yup, it's been moved, your were right. Now the {Item} is being housed near the {Location}. Someone is manipulating the situation, that's how it feels anyway. Keep your eyes open 
- D
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location005:
Joffrey - 
No, don't touch it! Just put the {Item} someplace safe. I recommend the {Location}, at least for now. 
- D
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location025:
Joffrey - 
Someone moved the {Item} from its safe spot. Please immediately return it to {Location}! If you find the rascals who moved it, hand over their names at once! 
- Cappy
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location031: Joffrey moved {Item}  to {Location}. It's a Magical Affairs case, I can't tell him what to do.
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Chambers.Primary4:
M - 
Everything is in the Chambers Vault as of this evening. Adjust your patrols accordingly. 
- W
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Chambers.Primary01:
M - 
He's supposed go hand it off during his dinner shifts. Now if anyone needs it, we gotta go all the way to the dining area. 
- W
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location008:
M - 
I moved that {Item} for ya. Should be safer now. Who's gonna look for it in the {Location}? Are we still on for tomorrow? 
- J
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.VariationA00:
M - 
I've reset the nightly code for the office safe. It's {Tower}. Eat this after you've read it. 
Brimsby.
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location015:
M - 
No, they keep moving it. Security reasons they say. The {Item} is in the {Location}. If you're gonna do something about it, now's the time. 
- J
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Primary09:
M - 
Spare key is in the Chief Constable's office. You owe me one. 
- W
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.VariationB00:
M - 
They hid the vault key in that safe, but rip up floor every time they move it! Why not give it to me and I'll do the job I'm paid for. 
- W
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.Vault003:
M- 
Bring aspirin. Climbing to the Tower Vault just to move that damned Effigy has just about turned my knees to porridge. 
- W
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Primary07:
M-  
Albert's got the key but he's hiding in the lounge. He's scared to death of the Effigy. 
- W
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location009:
MEMO 
As of tonight, the {Item} has been moved to a safe location. Be aware that it is now in the {Location}. Direct your questions to the Chief Constable, or Armory Head. 
End of memo.
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location024:
Margery - 
How my day flies when I am thinking of you! Yes, I found where you hid the {Item}. So let us have a game. I hid the thing, in this spot: {Location}. Happy hunting my sweet apple dumpling. 
- Seawell
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location012:
Margery - 
Thank you for asking. Yes, the {Location} seems a perfect spot for the {Item}. With admiration 
- Captain Seawell
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.VariationB00:
Mr. Hobart - 
Get a rug to cover the rips they left in my floor when they moved that safe. Oafs! Not a brain between them! 
- Mrs. Rowly
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.VariationA00:
Mr. Stote - 
I expect to see that the security devices are switched on in the hallway in front of the tower vault. They were left off all last night, I'm surprised nothing was stolen. I'm not blaming you, but I am *preparing* to blame you.  
- W

Mr. Stote - 
The security devices in the in the hallway iin front of the tower vault wer left off all last night, and while I'm not blaming you, I am preparing to blame you.  
- W
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location023:
My Darling Captain- 
I almost feel it's a little game with us, but I found the {Item}. I've hidden it in the {Location}. Was that wrong of me? This all feels a little wrong. In the most marvelous way. 
- Margery
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Primary08:
Nigel - 
Albert's got the key tonight, can you believe it? In the lounge all night. The thieves deserve this round.  
- Highsmith
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.Vault002:
Nigel - 
Take care when you move the Effigy to the Tower Vault. Those stairs are old and slippery.  
- Sergeant H
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location002: Night Shift - Be aware that the {Item} has been moved for security reasons by order of the Chief Constable. It will be kept at the {Location} until further notice.
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location011: Night Shift - Please stop moving the {Item}. We have placed it in the {Location} for safety reasons. Direct your complaints to the Chief Constable, see what that gets you.
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Chambers.Primary01: Notice to all Personnel: The switch for vault security is now located at in the boss' office. Address further inquiries to the Administrator's Office.
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Primary17: Notice to all personnel - the shut off switch to vault security is now located on the back wall of the boss's office. Please make a note of it. - Administrator's Office
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location029:
Oh John! Captain of my heart! 
I did find the {Item}, as you knew I would! So I've placed it somewhere clever: {Location}. Do find it and write to me, otherwise my day will be bleak and insufferably long. 
With affection, 
Margery
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Primary01:
Owens - 
That trick sconce in the library was supposed to be a secret, not a toy. I should have had it taken out years ago.
Carmichael
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location001: Oy, MacKara, tell the cappy I moved his precious {Item} to the {Location} for now. Why he don't put 'em all in his office I'll never know. - D
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Chambers.Primary9:
P - 
Still no answers about the Warburg Effigy. It's being housed in the Chambers Vault, so you know they're worried. 
- L
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Chambers.Primary2:
Please relocate all notable valuables to the Chambers Vault. Nothing else has the right level of shielding.
- Commissioner's Office
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.VariationA00: Psst. The safe's code in the office is {Tower} .
Toasts.QuestObjectiveUncomplete: Quest objective is no longer complete.
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Primary14: Reminder: The switch to toggle vault security is now located on the back wall of the office. All personnel should take note. - Manager's Office.
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Primary10:
Robinson - 
The Chief says he's handling the Warburg artifact personally. The key's in his office. 
- Clark
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Primary05:
S - 
Guess who has the vault key tonight? Yeah, mister lazy-bones, spends his shift at the lounge, is who. 
- K
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location022:
Seawell - 
I haven't seen any memoranda regarding the whereabouts of {Item}. Is it still in the {Location}? If so, please update the record. 
Thank you, 
Logan Avery
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Chambers.Primary3:
Shelly - 
Another change of plans. They've moved the valuables into the Chambers Vault for the duration. Safest place, if you ask me. 
- Brimsby
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Primary11:
Shelly - 
The Warburg Effigy's got a suspected enchantment. Chief Constable Avery is holding the key in his office to restrict access. I wouldn't fool with it.  - Brimsby
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.VariationA01:
Smythe - 
I just found out the Captain keeps a duplicate vault key inside in the office safe. What for? I only dropped it in the moat one time! 
- Verity
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.Vault008:
Smythe - 
It's good they've put the Effigy in the Tower Vault - thieves will pass out from the climb before they can snag it. 
- G
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Chambers.Primary02:
Smythe - 
You should only turn off vault security in a dire emergency. But if you absolutely must, the switch is located in the boss' office. Understood? 
- Crabapple
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Chambers.Primary8:
Smythe - 
You're right, something's sketchy about that Bavarian item. Safest place for it is the Chambers Vault for now. 
- Crabapple
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Intro: Steal all the gems needed to complete the Staff of Edinburgh
Toasts.SubquestObjectiveUncomplete: Subquest objective is no longer complete.
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.VariationA01:
T - 
The Captain says I should keep the Vault Tower key close to my chest, even on basement rounds. What's he worried about? Pickpocket rats? 
- G
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.Vault006:
TO ALL - 
The Warburg Effigy is being placed in the Tower Vault. Maybe the climb will get some of you back into shape. 
- Sergent H
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Vault9: TO ALL - The Warburg Effigy is not a toy. We put it in the Archives vault for a reason. - Sergeant H
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Vault1: The Archivist wants a look at that statue before it goes to London. Check the Archives for it.
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Vault4: The Effigy's upstairs in the Archives for the night. Something about Bavarian sorcery.  Steer clear. - Nigel

Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location034: The {Item}  goes in {Location}. Tell Seawell it's an Aggravated Thaumaturgical case, he's not qualified.
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Fact.Archives.Key2: There is a spare key in the Chief Constable's office
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Primary19:
There's a heap of health packs in the bathroom if you need them.
-GM
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.VariationA00: They're keeping the key to the vault in the office safe? Mighty secure. Makes one wonder what they have up there, right Worthington? - Lester
Toasts.Guildhall.Set.Location014:
Update: Uniform inspection day will now be Tuesdays, this includes yer boots, lads. Also, per the captain's orders the {Item} is currently being held at the {Location}. 
Constable Duffy
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.VariationB00:
W - 
Did you see those scrapes they dug in the floor moving that safe? If anyone was wondering where we kept it, it's no secret now! 
- Cappy
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Chambers.Primary02:
W - 
Did you see where they put the vault security switch? We had to get Cappy to come down and show us. It's in the boss' office. I mean, why there? 
- M
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Chambers.Primary6:
W - 
Have you heard? That Bavarian piece is being kept in the Chambers Vault. Something's off about it. 
- M
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.Vault009:
W - 
The Warburg Effigy is in the Tower Vault now. It was eating into my nap time, talking away like it does! 
- M
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Chambers.Primary01:
W - 
There's something off with the horse painting in the Chief Constable's office. Have Magical Affairs look at it, and open the safe some other way.
- M
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.VariationA01:
Wallace - 
I don't envy Mr. Gregor. Basement patrol's bad enough, but holding on to the vault key while you do it? He's a walking target! 
- M
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Tower.Vault007:
Wallace - 
So I'm assigned to the Tower Vault till Friday. Let me know if you want me to ask the Effegy anything! 
- Brisby
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Chambers.Primary01:
Worthington - 
That horse painting in the director's office isn't what it looks like. 
- Barnaby
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Vault3: Yes, the Archives are a secure location. Pay us a visit if you don't believe me. Truthfully, Wincham, Assistant to the Chief Archivist
Toasts.Guildhall.Procurement.Archives.Primary18:
Your loot's no good to you if you get knocked out. Our man inside has left some health packs in the bathroom.
The Guild
